hints.securis ad: 'securis' is a noun (axe) and 'ad' is a preposition — neither is a verb
hints.Iam securis: 'Iam' is an adverb (already) and 'securis' is a noun (axe)
hints.radicem posita: 'radicem' is a noun (root) and 'posita' is part of the verb compound 'posita est'

steps.prompt: The infinitive of 'posita est' translates to?

hints.to be able: 'to be able' is 'posse' — 'posita est' comes from 'ponere' (to place/put)
hints.to ask: 'to ask' is 'ponere' means 'to place', not 'to ask' ('rogare'/'poscere' = to ask)

steps.prompt: What tense and voice is 'posita est'?

hints.Present passive: Present passive would be 'ponitur' — 'posita est' (participle + est) is perfect passive
hints.Future passive: Future passive would be 'ponetur' — 'posita est' is perfect passive
hints.Pluperfect passive: Pluperfect passive would be 'posita erat' — 'posita est' uses 'est' (perfect)

hints.possum, posse, potui, —: These are the parts of 'posse' (to be able)
hints.possideo, possidere, possedi, possessum: These are the parts of 'possidere' (to possess)
hints.pasco, pascere, pavi, pastum: These are the parts of 'pascere' (to feed/pasture)

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'securim' — 'securis' is nominative, the subject
hints.Genitive: Genitive singular is also 'securis' (same form), but here it is the subject — nominative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'securis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'securis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'securis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'radix' — 'radicem' with -em is accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'radice' — 'radicem' is accusative after 'ad'
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'radici' — 'radicem' is accusative governed by 'ad'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'radicis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'radicis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'radicis' ends in -is

translation: Already the axe is laid to the root
